title: Hash Tables, Dictionaries, Sets, and Collision Policy · Structure Brief: Adversarial Hashing
summary: Create a deduplication and lookup service that remains correct under collision-heavy data. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dsa adversarial hashing increment in a hash-table model and workload benchmark. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test collision stress and avoid claims based only on average benign keys.
Use dictionaries, sets, counters, and default mappings according to semantic need.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.code:
# Worked focus: dsa-adversarial-hashing — Test collision stress and avoid claims based only on average benign keys.
class CollisionKey:
    def __init__(self, value: str): self.value = value
    def __hash__(self): return 4
    def __eq__(self, other): return isinstance(other, CollisionKey) and self.value == other.value

index = {CollisionKey("aa"): 1, CollisionKey("bb"): 2}
assert len(index) == 2
